meta.name: power-platform-connector
meta.title: Power Platform Connector Skill
meta.description: Comprehensive development guidelines for Power Platform Custom Connectors using JSON Schema definitions. Covers API definitions (Swagger 2.0), API properties, and settings configuration with Microsoft

actions.description: Connector Metadata: This includes publisher information, capabilities, and branding elements.

actions.description: `x-ms-visibility`: Use this to control parameter visibility with values of `important`, `advanced`, or `internal`.

actions.description: `x-ms-trigger-metadata`: Use this to define trigger configuration settings including kind and mode properties.

actions.description: `x-ms-no-generic-test`: Use this to disable automatic testing for specific operations.

actions.name: `x-ms-operation-context`: Use this to configure operation si
actions.description: `x-ms-operation-context`: Use this to configure operation simulation settings for testing purposes.

actions.name: `x-ms-dynamic-values`: Use this to configure dynamic value s
actions.description: `x-ms-dynamic-values`: Use this to configure dynamic value sources that populate parameter options.

rules.description: API version specifications to ensure compatibility with Power Platform services.
1. Always validate against Swagger 2.0 spec - The schema enforces strict Swagger 2.0 compliance
`x-ms-summary`: Use this to provide user-friendly display names and ensure you use title case formatting.
`x-ms-safe-operation`: Use this to mark POST operations as safe when they don't have side effects.
`x-ms-test-value`: Use this to provide sample values for testing, but never include secrets or sensitive data.
You should define appropriate `securityDefinitions` for your API to ensure proper authentication.
You should use `oauth2` for modern APIs, `apiKey` for simple token authentication, and consider `basic` auth only for internal/legacy systems.
Each security definition must be exactly one type (this constraint is enforced by oneOf validation).
You should use descriptive `description` fields to help users understand each parameter's purpose.
You should implement `x-ms-summary` for better user experience (title case is required).

directives.description: This workspace contains JSON Schema definitions for Power Platform Custom Connectors, specifically for the `paconn` (Power Apps Connector) tool. The s
Purpose: This file contains Swagger 2.0 API definitions with Power Platform extensions.
Standard Swagger 2.0 properties including info, paths, definitions, and more.
Microsoft-specific extensions that begin with `x-ms-*` prefixes.
Security definitions that support OAuth2, API Key, and Basic Auth authentication methods.
Environment GUID targeting for specific Power Platform environments.
`x-ms-trigger`: Use this to mark operations as triggers with values of `batch` or `single`.
